justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Pre-commit offset pattern explicitly identified as fatal flaw causing silent data loss on crash — called out as the 'single highest-severity finding' and a 'well-known Kafka anti-pattern'. (2) Clear explanation that retry logic and DLQ do not protect against pre-commit crash — 'Events lost to crashes never reach the DLQ — they simply vanish' and 'The 3-retry logic only covers in-process failures'. (3) Verdict is NOT READY. (4) Specific remediation provided: switch to manual offset commit (enable.auto.commit=false), commit after processing, make downstream idempotent. (5) Observability correctly noted as adequate baseline — logging, metrics, health check acknowledged as sound foundation, not used to justify approval.

observations.text: Precisely identifies the commit-before-process anti-pattern as the critical finding and correctly labels it as converting at-least-once to at-most-once semantics
Explicitly states DLQ is undermined by early offset commit — events lost to crashes never reach the DLQ
Provides actionable remediation: enable.auto.commit=false, move commit after processing, add idempotency
Escalation correctly routed as a deployment blocker, not a nice-to-have
Identifies a secondary fatal interaction: DLQ produce failure also loses events due to early offset commit
Observability review is balanced — acknowledges good baseline while noting real gaps without using them to compensate for the offset bug
Strong additional analysis: identifies missing processing timeout, missing DLQ replay tooling, and unclear circuit breaker behavior during open state

agent_output_excerpt: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"Kafka consumer","to":"Kafka broker (payment.events)","status":"broken","issue":"Offset is committed immediately after receive, BEFORE processing completes. If the service crashes or processing fails after commit, the event is permanently lost — Kafka will never redeliver it. The 3-retry logic only covers in-process failures; a crash between commit and processing completion causes silent data loss on financial events."},{"from":"Kafk
